description_long: This irregularly shaped stone features a predominantly white surface with patches of gray and dark streaks, giving it a mottled, weathered appearance. The stone has a rough, uneven texture with visible indentations and a few shallow grooves, suggesting natural erosion or impact marks. One side displays a prominent elongated depression, while the opposite side is more rounded and bulbous. The surface is matte and non-reflective, with a tactile hardness typical of dense sedimentary or metamorphic rock. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a substantial obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or roadside setting. The stone's coloration and surface features indicate exposure to the elements, with no visible artificial modifications.
description: A rough, irregular white and gray stone with mottled surface patterns and natural indentations. Its weathered, matte finish and substantial size make it a prominent urban obstacle or decorative feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears elongated with a sloping top and a mix of white and gray patches, showing a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights a broad, mostly white face with a distinct elongated depression and subtle gray mottling.
description_view_2: Seen from the side, the stone's irregular profile and layered color transitions are visible, emphasizing its thickness and rough edges.
description_view_3: The back reveals a more rounded, bulbous surface with pronounced gray and white mottling and a deep groove.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern with irregular white and gray patches, some elongated grooves and natural indentations
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible surface erosion and minor pitting, indicating prolonged exposure to outdoor elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban environments, may partially blend with natural landscaping or be surrounded by low vegetation
